title: vicia disperma var. suberviformis
intro: Vicia disperma var. suberviformis, also known as the sub-erect pea vetch, is a variety of the vetch plant that is native to parts of Europe and Asia. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is valued for its ability to clear heat and toxic substances from the body, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The health benefits of Vicia disperma var. suberviformis include relief from fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of the plant may include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water to release their medicinal properties.
